tasks.description: Enable 4 new export sections (flip available:false → true for executiveSummary, journeyMap, journeySentiment, perspectiveComparison). Also fix IMP-096 (Executive Summary preset config — should only enable canvasSnapshot + gapAnalysis + executiveSummary, not dataTable + costAnalysis) and IMP-095 (auto-switch to Custom preset when user manually toggles a checkbox after selecting a preset).
acceptance_criteria: executiveSummary, journeyMap, journeySentiment, perspectiveComparison all have available: true in SECTION_GROUPS
All 4 sections appear enabled (no 'coming soon' badge) in the Export PDF dialog
Selecting 'Executive Summary' preset only enables canvasSnapshot, gapAnalysis, and executiveSummary — NOT dataTable or costAnalysis
Toggling any checkbox while a preset is active auto-switches the active preset indicator to show no preset is active (or 'Custom' if a custom state exists)

patterns_to_follow: SECTION_GROUPS array has { key, label, available } objects
Preset configs are typed ExportConfig objects at module scope

tasks.special_instructions: Lines 101, 102, 103, 109, 110, 111, 112, 113: change available: false to available: true for executiveSummary, journeyMap, journeySentiment, perspectiveComparison. Lines 102-103 (improvements, aiInsights) should stay available: false — those render functions don't exist yet (being built in slot 2). For IMP-096: update EXECUTIVE_SUMMARY_CONFIG to only set canvasSnapshot, gapAnalysis, executiveSummary to true, everything else false. For IMP-095: in handleToggle or wherever checkbox change is handled, clear the active preset state so no preset button appears selected.

tasks.description: Build 4 new PDF render functions: renderPrioritizationMatrix, renderToolLandscape, renderImprovements, renderAIInsights. Wire them into handleEnhancedExportPdf in canvas-view.tsx. These sections use existing data types (Step with effort_score/impact_score, Tool with status/monthly_cost, ImprovementIdea with status/priority, AIAnalysisResult from workspace settings).
acceptance_criteria: renderPrioritizationMatrix function exists and renders steps with effort_score/impact_score in a table or quadrant summary
renderToolLandscape function exists and renders tools grouped by status with cost summary
renderImprovements function exists and renders improvement ideas grouped by status/priority
renderAIInsights function exists and renders AI analysis results (bottlenecks, redundancies, automation, general)
All 4 functions are wired into handleEnhancedExportPdf in canvas-view.tsx with lazy data fetching
Export config keys prioritizationMatrix, toolLandscape, improvements, aiInsights control inclusion

similar_files: src/lib/export/enhanced-pdf-sections.ts (existing 4 render functions to match style)
patterns_to_follow: Each render function: adds new page, renders section header, renders content tables/cards, returns void
Data interfaces defined at top of enhanced-pdf-sections.ts
canvas-view.tsx handleEnhancedExportPdf: lazy-imports enhanced-pdf-sections, fetches data conditionally based on config, calls render functions in sequence
Fetch data from API client functions (fetchImprovementIdeas, fetchTools, etc.) — check client.ts for existing wrappers
learnings_relevant: apiFetch unwraps envelope — returns T directly

tasks.special_instructions: Follow the exact style of the existing 4 render functions in enhanced-pdf-sections.ts. Use the same PDF layout patterns (margins, fonts, colors, table rendering). For AI insights, the data is stored in workspace.settings.last_analysis (JSONB). For prioritization, use steps with effort_score/impact_score. For tools, use Tool[] with status/monthly_cost. For improvements, use ImprovementIdea[]. All data should be fetched lazily in canvas-view.tsx only when the corresponding config flag is true. After building render functions, flip the remaining 4 sections to available: true in export-pdf-dialog.tsx — BUT WAIT: export-pdf-dialog.tsx is owned by slot 1. Instead, add these sections to available:true ONLY in enhanced-pdf-sections.ts exports and canvas-view.tsx wiring. Slot 1 is NOT flipping these 4 to true (they stay false until render functions exist). Once this slot builds the render functions, the sections need to be enabled. RESOLUTION: This slot should ALSO modify export-pdf-dialog.tsx to flip improvements, aiInsights, prioritizationMatrix, toolLandscape to available: true. OWNERSHIP CONFLICT — moving export-pdf-dialog.tsx to shared_files with slot 1 as owner. Slot 2 must NOT modify export-pdf-dialog.tsx. Instead, leave those 4 as available: false for now — they'll be enabled in the next iteration once render functions are verified.

tasks.description: Add aria-label to color picker input in coloring panel for WCAG 1.3.1 compliance.
acceptance_criteria: input[type='color'] in coloring-panel.tsx has aria-label='Rule color picker' or similar descriptive label
Associated hex text input has aria-label describing its purpose

patterns_to_follow: Use aria-label on native input elements per WCAG 1.3.1

tasks.special_instructions: Simple a11y fix. Find the <input type='color'> element(s) in coloring-panel.tsx and add aria-label. Also check if the associated hex text input needs an aria-label.

shared_files.note: export-pdf-dialog.tsx is owned by slot 1 (#BUG-040). Slot 2 may READ it but must NOT modify it. The 4 new sections (improvements, aiInsights, prioritizationMatrix, toolLandscape) will remain available: false until next iteration when render functions are verified.
